summary: Iteration 2 frontend e2e — /assets/new route fix VERIFIED (form renders correctly). Mobile hides Tech Specs, Laptop shows it. Auto-generated asset_id (LAP-0003) works. Existing-asset locked/edit/save/cancel/delete cycle works. Maintenance global (new-maint-btn) and inline on asset detail both add+delete work. Assets tabs (5), search, dept-filter all functional. Settings change-password toasts correct (wrong current, mismatch, success), Admin2 password rotate + reset verified. Logout returns to /login. ONE CRITICAL BUG: after saving a NEW asset, the form stays in EDIT mode (editing state not reset) — edit-btn not shown, save-btn still visible; page reload shows locked correctly.

ui_bugs.issue: After POST /assets succeeds and navigate(`/assets/${data.id}`) runs, `editing` state stays true so the form remains editable instead of switching to LOCKED read-only mode. Test req expects edit-btn visible and no inputs after save. Reloading the page fixes it (component remounts with isNew=false → editing=false), which proves the only missing piece is a setEditing(false) call in the isNew branch of save(). Repro: /assets/new → pick any category → fill fields → Save → stays on edit form with save-btn visible, no Lock badge, no edit-btn.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/pages/AssetDetail.jsx inside save(), isNew branch: after navigate(`/assets/${data.id}`) add `setEditing(false);` (and optionally setOriginal(normalized) using the response) so the just-created asset immediately renders in LOCKED mode as the spec requires.

Optional: the 2× 401 console errors observed on /login are from the initial GET /api/auth/me probe before login — not a functional bug, but could be silenced by treating 401 as expected on the login page.
critical_code_review_comments: AssetDetail.save() isNew path calls navigate() but does not reset `editing` to false. The useEffect re-runs loadAsset on new id, but state `editing` was initialized to isNew (true) and never gets reset when id transitions from 'new' → uuid. Must explicitly setEditing(false) after the navigate in the isNew branch.
Consider unifying maintenance 'add' testids between the two pages (global page uses `new-maint-btn`/`dlg-maint-*`, asset detail uses `add-maintenance-btn`/`maint-*`) — minor but easier for testers.
AssetDetail loadRefs and loadAsset both run on id change; loadRefs reloads employees/categories/locations unnecessarily on every id change. Memoise or gate on first mount only.
Dept filter on /assets works but the dropdown only showed 2 options in my run (likely 'All Departments' + one existing dept). Seed data may not include diverse departments; not a bug.

success_rate.frontend: ~93% — 13/14 flows pass; only new-asset→locked-mode transition broken

test_credentials: Admin/nothing and Admin2/nothing2 (both work; Admin2 used for the live change-password round-trip and was reset back to nothing2 at the end)
seed_data_creation: Created/deleted multiple TEST assets (LAP-0003 left existing with AcmeEditTest after successful edit-save test). One TESTE2E_<ts> employee was also left from a creation attempt. These are benign test artefacts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Only remaining blocker is the new-asset→locked-mode transition. After main agent adds setEditing(false) in AssetDetail.jsx save() isNew branch, re-verify: (1) POST /assets via UI → edit-btn appears, save-btn disappears without page reload, (2) Lock icon/'LOCKED' badge visible in header. All other flows (login, tabs, search, dept-filter, maintenance global+inline, settings change-password incl. success path, logout) are green. Test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py unchanged.
